qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Recursive normalize() causes stack overflow on deeply nested XML (CWE-674)
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies a stack overflow in the recursive Node.normalize() implementation (lib/dom.js:1313-1325) triggered by deeply nested XML. The advisory describes XML node injection via unvalidated processing instruction data containing ?> in createProcessingInstruction (lib/dom.js:2240-2246) and the PI serializer case (lib/dom.js:2966). Although both affect @xmldom/xmldom, the vulnerable functions, root causes, and impact classes differ entirely: unbounded recursion vs. missing input validation for PI-closing delimiters.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability: XML node injection through unvalidated processing instruction serialization in createProcessingInstruction/PI-serializer in @xmldom/xmldom. The 'normalize-stack-overflow' finding identifies a real but distinct vulnerability (recursive normalize stack overflow) in the same package with a different root cause (missing depth limit vs. missing PI delimiter validation), vulnerable function, and impact class (DoS vs. XML injection).
evidence: Advisory root cause: createProcessingInstruction stores data without validating ?> (lib/dom.js:2240-2246), serializer emits node.data verbatim in PI case (lib/dom.js:2966).

normalize-stack-overflow finding: describes unbounded recursion in Node.normalize() (lib/dom.js:1313-1325) causing stack overflow. Different vulnerable function, different root cause (missing depth limit vs. missing PI delimiter validation), different impact (DoS vs. XML injection).
